businessSummary.oneLiner: Decentralized digital asset with a hard-capped 21M supply — the dominant store-of-value crypto by market cap
businessSummary.description: Bitcoin is a SHA-256 proof-of-work blockchain launched in January 2009, with a fixed maximum supply of 21M coins that has positioned it as a digital inflation hedge. The April 2024 fourth halving cut block subsidy to 3.125 BTC and reduced daily new supply to ~450 BTC. Spot ETF launches in January 2024 (BlackRock IBIT, Fidelity FBTC, Bitwise BITB) opened a structural channel for institutional inflows. After printing an all-time high of $126,080 in December 2025, BTC corrected ~38% on macro tightening fears and profit-taking, then rebounded +17.5% over the past 30 days to reclaim its 50-day SMA at $71,809. BTC dominance sits near 55%, and network hash rate is at all-time highs.
howTheyMakeMoney: Block subsidy: miners earn 3.125 BTC per block
Transaction fees: scale with block-space demand and grow in importance after halvings
Layer-2 settlement (Lightning, Stacks, BitVM): payments and DeFi use cases
Spot ETF managers: 0.20-0.25% custody fees absorb sustained inflows

keyMetrics.name: Spot Price
keyMetrics.interpretation: -38.3% from ATH, trading below the 200-day SMA at $84,758. Mid-cycle correction zone

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Roughly the 7th largest asset globally. Only ~11% the size of gold ($14T), leaving meaningful adoption headroom

keyMetrics.name: 30-Day Change
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reclaim of the 50-day SMA signals a near-term trend shift; SMA200 has not yet been recovered

keyMetrics.name: 1-Year Change
keyMetrics.interpretation: Lagging prior post-halving cycle averages (+200% YoY) but realized-cap losses for holders remain limited

keyMetrics.name: RSI(14)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral-to-bullish. A push above 70 would raise short-term profit-taking risk

keyMetrics.name: Circulating / Max Supply
keyMetrics.interpretation: Over 95% of all bitcoin that will ever exist has been mined. Marginal new-supply impact is small and shrinking

keyMetrics.name: Turnover (24h Vol / Mcap)
keyMetrics.interpretation: In line with major asset classes — deep liquidity, low slippage even for size

growthDrivers.title: Spot ETF cumulative net inflows cross $60B; institutional allocation models adopt BTC
growthDrivers.description: U.S. spot ETFs (IBIT, FBTC, BITB) have gathered ~$60B in cumulative net inflows since launch. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley PB channels now recommend a 1-3% standard BTC weight in model portfolios. ETF demand averages 2-3x the new-supply rate (~160K BTC/yr ≈ $12B), meaningfully tilting the supply-demand balance

growthDrivers.title: U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (SBR) legislation in motion
growthDrivers.description: Following the November 2025 executive order, Texas and Florida advanced state-level BTC reserve budgets in early 2026. Federal SBR legislation could authorize purchases of up to 1M BTC if passed — but timing and scale remain politically contingent

growthDrivers.title: Lightning · Taproot drive payment and DeFi utility
growthDrivers.description: Lightning Network channel capacity has crossed 8,000 BTC with payment integrations on Telegram and X. Taproot asset issuance and BitVM2 verification expand on-Bitcoin smart contracts — promoting BTC from pure SoV to settlement and financial infrastructure

growthDrivers.title: Halving cycle base case still supportive
growthDrivers.description: Historical halving cycles (2012, 2016, 2020) saw new ATHs 12-18 months post-halving followed by cyclical corrections. The December 2025 ATH lines up with this rhythm relative to the April 2024 halving, leaving room for further upside into mid-2026 — though halving impact is marginally diminishing

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Hard-capped supply, the most decentralized validator set, and the longest security track record give Bitcoin a near-monopoly on the digital store-of-value category

moats.description: The 21M supply cap is enforced in code and has held for 16 years without modification — trustless scarcity that no central bank can replicate
Network hash rate sits above 700 EH/s at all-time highs. Capital required for a 51% attack is estimated at $30B+, making the economic cost dwarf any rational attack incentive
16 years of uninterrupted operation across multiple bear markets and exchange failures. Markets increasingly classify BTC as its own asset class rather than as 'crypto'
Spot ETFs in the U.S. and Europe, plus institutional custodians (Coinbase Custody, Fidelity Digital), are now standardized. Other coins would need years of regulatory track record to match

competitors.name: Gold
competitors.detail: Traditional inflation hedge. ~$14T market cap, ~9x BTC. Bitcoin is gaining share as 'digital gold'

competitors.name: Ethereum (ETH)
competitors.detail: Leading smart-contract platform but with variable monetary policy. Loses to BTC on the SoV narrative — ETH/BTC ratio at 0.038 is near a 5-year low

competitors.name: Stablecoins (USDT, USDC)
competitors.detail: Dollar-pegged assets capture short-term liquidity demand but do not substitute for BTC as an inflation hedge or long-term savings vehicle

recentNews.headline: BlackRock IBIT crosses $25B cumulative net inflows — fastest ETF on record
recentNews.significance: Reaffirms institutional demand structure. Daily average inflows running near $80M

recentNews.headline: Texas legislature passes $100M strategic Bitcoin reserve budget
recentNews.significance: First state-level BTC reserve — adds momentum to federal SBR legislation

recentNews.headline: Fed May FOMC consensus: hold; July first-cut probability rises
recentNews.significance: Weak-dollar and falling-real-rate expectations are constructive for BTC

recentNews.headline: Lightning Network channel capacity surpasses 8,000 BTC for the first time
recentNews.significance: Payment infrastructure adoption — supports BTC as more than a speculative asset

analystOpinions.notableComment: Standard Chartered and Bernstein keep $150K-$200K targets for end-2026. JPMorgan warns of a $60K retest if ETF inflows stall

risks.title: Prolonged macro tightening
risks.description: If the Fed delays cuts or pivots to additional hikes on inflation reacceleration, risk assets broadly come under pressure. BTC, sensitive to real rates, could see a -30% drawdown scenario

risks.title: ETF inflows stall or reverse to net outflows
risks.description: The single biggest current bull driver. Daily net buys falling below $30M, or turning to net outflows, would accelerate near-term selling — particularly around large derivatives expirations

risks.title: Regulatory and tax changes
risks.description: Capital gains and transfer taxes in non-U.S. G20 jurisdictions could expand. Some countries are reviewing mining bans. Unlikely to cause immediate price shocks but pressure the long-term adoption curve

risks.title: Exchange or custodian failure
risks.description: An FTX (2022)- or Mt.Gox (2014)-class incident would deliver a short-term confidence shock. Systemic risk is lower than past cycles thanks to ETF and institutional custody

risks.title: Protocol-level security event
risks.description: Quantum threats or critical code vulnerabilities are the largest tail risks, but near-term probability is low. The community is already preparing quantum-resistant signature schemes

buyReasons.title: Reclaimed SMA50 after a -38% correction from ATH
buyReasons.rationale: +17.5% over 30 days suggests a near-term trend shift, but until SMA200 is recovered it is more sensible to use this as a scaling-in zone than a trend-following setup

buyReasons.title: Structural supply scarcity
buyReasons.rationale: Circulating supply at 95.3%, annual issuance ~0.85% — stock-to-flow now rivals gold. With even modest sustained demand, time favors Bitcoin

buyReasons.title: Irreversible institutional adoption
buyReasons.rationale: ETFs, custody, FASB accounting treatment, and bank custody are all in place. Asset classes that gain institutional infrastructure rarely disappear

buyReasons.title: Macro turning supportive
buyReasons.rationale: Entering a Fed cutting cycle and weaker-dollar environment is positive-beta for BTC. Timing depends on macro data, however

overallOpinion: BTC has bounced from a mid-cycle correction but has yet to clear the 200-day SMA — a textbook 'basing' phase.
Given supply structure and the irreversibility of institutional adoption, the price zone is attractive for long-term holders, though near-term it remains tied to macro variables.
Key swing factors are ETF flows and Fed policy. As long as both stay supportive, recovery toward the $105K consensus target is the reasonable base case.

investmentVerdict.summary: Confirmed near-term bounce after a deep correction from ATH. As long as supply scarcity and institutional adoption momentum persist, scaling in is reasonable — but until SMA200 is reclaimed, build the position gradually within a 1-5% portfolio weight
